title: Athyrium biseriale
taxonomy: Athyrium biseriale is classified as a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. This genus is a group of ferns commonly known as lady ferns or lady-ferns. The Athyrium biseriale belongs to the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. It is native to certain regions and can be identified by its distinctive leaf shape and size. Further classification details include its kingdom and phylum, which are Plantae and Pteridophyta, respectively.
uses: Athyrium biseriale is known to have various medicinal uses. The plant's leaves have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal benefits and uses of Athyrium biseriale.

intro: Athyrium biseriale, also known as the two-pinnate lady fern, is a species of fern native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium biseriale include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Athyrium biseriale is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is commonly used to make teas, capsules, and tablets to support overall health and well-being.
